destinations.evidence: Routine wall-clock cleanup has no Scheduler job eviction path.
destinations.snippet: Routine stale_eviction_tick removes queued_transactions older than max_tx_lifetime and async lifecycle entries; raw scheduled txpool admission is dormant.

destinations.evidence: Future scheduled instances come from Scheduler state, not a txpool scheduled job cache.
destinations.snippet: compute_obligations/read_active_scheduled_calls derive work from Scheduler contract parent state.

destinations.evidence: Surviving Scheduler jobs can produce future instances after routine txpool cleanup.
destinations.snippet: Payload builder scans Scheduler state and synthesizes PayloadCandidate::Scheduled for due obligations.

destinations.evidence: Verifier parity also treats Scheduler parent state as source of truth.
destinations.snippet: Verifier recomputes scheduled obligations from parent state.

equivalence_argument: Routine max_tx_lifetime cleanup cannot delete Scheduler jobs in v2 because scheduled jobs are not owned by txpool; due scheduled transactions are re-derived from Scheduler parent state.

related_but_insufficient_code: No scheduled pool file/API exists in audited port; Alloy/revm own primitives/effects, not Scheduler job eviction.

rationale: The later candidates explicitly remove the prior scheduler.evict_call loop from max-lifetime expiry and preserve Scheduler jobs unless chain halt has been detected.
statement: At current tip, max_tx_lifetime expiry without chain-halt detection removes expired scheduled transaction instances from the pool but leaves the corresponding Scheduler jobs available to produce future eligible transaction instances.
suggested_falsification_tests: With no chain halt detected, insert a scheduled transaction whose timestamp exceeds max_tx_lifetime, tick maintenance, and assert the transaction is removed from the scheduled pool but SynchronousScheduler still has the call ID.
Repeat with chain halt detected and assert the call ID is evicted, proving the distinction between routine age expiry and poison recovery.
summary: Routine scheduled expiry preserves Scheduler jobs structurally by not owning/evicting them in txpool.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Age out any stale queued instance, tick maintenance, then build/verify later block and assert compute_obligations still produces Scheduler state call/index.
title: Routine scheduled expiry preserves Scheduler jobs
